title: asplenium ceterach subsp. bivalens
intro: Asplenium ceterach subsp. bivalens is a fern species within the Aspleniaceae family, commonly known as the 'bivalve spleenwort' due to its unique two-lobed frond. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Asplenium ceterach subsp. bivalens include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
